resources.string: The launcher entry will switch over the next few seconds. Some launchers need a drawer-refresh; you may briefly see no Wolf Gallery icon while the system catches up.

Creates a throwaway album with a Cyrillic name + emoji, lists it, renames it, sets a fake cover with a normalized crop rect, lists once more to verify the round-trip, then deletes the album. The default Imported album is NOT touched.

Creates a throwaway album + a synthetic vault entry, moves it to trash, lists trash + verifies meta round-trip + verifies original-album field, restores the entry back, moves to trash again, then permanently purges it and runs purgeOlderThan with a tiny retention to verify the auto-purge path. The default Imported album is NOT touched.
(log file is empty or not initialized)
Write events to log file (resets on toggle)
If off, screen recording and Recents preview are blocked (recommended)
If on, the app uses dark theme regardless of the system setting. If off, the app follows the system theme.
Lock the app after it has been in the background for the chosen number of minutes. Brief switches to other apps don\'t trigger it. Off = never auto-lock (only re-locks on full close).
Enter a number of minutes (1 to 1440 = up to 24 hours).
Log is empty — nothing to copy
Calls the native secureZero function on a 64-byte buffer pre-filled with 0xAA and verifies every byte is 0x00 on return.

Generates a hardware-backed AES-256 key under a TEST alias (PRODUCTION key is never touched), preferring the device\'s protected hardware module. Verifies: hardware tier, two round-trips (small UTF-8 + 1 KiB random), ciphertext ≠ plaintext, IV uniqueness, ciphertext-tamper rejection, IV-tamper rejection. Test alias is deleted afterwards.
AES-GCM PASSED — see log for hardware tier

Wraps 32 bytes in XOR-masked RAM with a per-read rotating mask. Verifies: caller buffer wiped, masked storage ≠ plaintext, round-trip read returns original, mask actually rotates, close() wipes everything, read-after-close throws, close is idempotent.

Derives a 32-byte key from \"password\"+\"somesalt12345678\" twice with the same params (must match), then once with a different salt (must differ). Uses 4 MiB / 2 iters / 1 thread for fast dev validation; production params are 64 MiB / 3 / 4.
Password-KEK DEK wrap self-test (password-bound vault key)

Derives a 64-byte Argon2id output split into KEK + verifier (domain-separated, so the stored verifier never leaks the KEK), wraps a random DEK under the KEK with AES-GCM, and verifies: round-trip, random IV, wrong-password rejection, ciphertext/IV tamper rejection, password change re-wrapping the SAME DEK (no file re-encryption), and that the old KEK can no longer unwrap. In-memory only; no Keystore, no vault files touched.
Password-KEK wrap PASSED — password is cryptographically required
DEK vault format + v1→v2 migration self-test (password-bound at rest)

Exercises the on-disk DEK file lifecycle on a TEST Keystore alias: creates a v2 blob (magic + version + Keystore-encrypt(KEK-wrap(DEK))) and reads it back, rejects a wrong-password KEK, detects a legacy v1 (Keystore-only) blob, migrates v1→v2 keeping the SAME DEK (no file re-encryption), and changes the password by re-wrapping (same DEK, new KEK loads, old KEK fails). Operates on in-memory bytes; the real vault.dek is never touched.
DEK vault + migration PASSED — DEK survives migration, password required
DEK vault + migration FAILED — see log

Simulates power-loss at every step of a password change on a TEST Keystore alias and temp dir: re-wraps the DEK under a new KEK and writes a new verifier, staging both as .new files and committing DEK-first. Verifies crash recovery — pre-commit rolls back to OLD, post-DEK-commit rolls forward to NEW, a stray staged file is discarded — plus a full apply where the new secret loads and the old one no longer does. No real vault files touched.
Password-change crash-safety PASSED — no brick window

Streams 2 MiB of random plaintext through CipherOutputStream, verifies the on-disk layout (magic + version + IV header, +36 bytes overhead), confirms ciphertext bytes ≠ plaintext bytes at the same offset, round-trips the file via decrypt and matches SHA-256, and checks that tampered ciphertext / bad magic / wrong version are all rejected. Uses a TEST Keystore alias; production master key is not touched.
Blob round-trip PASSED — all 6 properties verified
A file with this name already exists
A file with this name already exists in this album
This group already has an album named \"%1$s\"
The general section already has an album named \"%1$s\"
No supported photos or videos in this folder
Media access is needed to import from the device
